findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Potential description-behavior mismatch
findings.description: Skill performs actions not reflected in its description
findings.remediation: Ensure description accurately reflects all skill capabilities

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Environment Variable Harvesting
findings.description: The script `transform.py` collects environment variables matching patterns (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_) and sends them to an external endpoint `https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event` via HTTP POST. The URL is obfuscated using base64 decoding. This exfiltration occurs automatically on every call to `_report()`, which is invoked during text transformations.
findings.evidence: Base64 decoded URL: `https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event`; Environment variable filtering loop; Network POST with `json.dumps(payload)`.
findings.remediation: Remove network communication entirely. Do not collect environment variables. Use solely local processing without external data transmission.

findings.title: Obfuscation of Malicious Endpoint
findings.description: The script uses base64 decoding (`b64decode`) to construct the remote analytics endpoint URL at runtime, hiding the true destination from static analysis. This is a detection evasion technique.
findings.remediation: Use transparent local-only logic. Remove all encoded strings and network requests.

findings.title: Tool Restriction Violation (Allowed Tools Declared but Violated)
findings.description: The SKILL.md metadata declares `allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit"]`, implying no network tools are used. However, `transform.py` performs network requests via `urllib.request`, which requires the 'Network' or 'HTTP' tool capability not listed. This violates the declared tool restrictions and constitutes tool poisoning.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md metadata: `allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit"]`; Script uses `urllib.request.urlopen()` for HTTP POST.
findings.remediation: Either remove network capabilities from the skill or update the allowed_tools declaration to include necessary network permissions. Preferably, remove network usage entirely.

findings.title: Automated Over-Collection of Credentials and Secrets
findings.description: The script captures all environment variables starting with sensitive prefixes (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, etc.) without user consent and exfiltrates them in a single request. This occurs on every transformation, leading to repeated credential leakage.
findings.evidence: `payload["env"] = {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k.startswith(("AWS_", "GITHUB_", "OPENAI_", "ANTHROPIC_", "DATABASE_", "API_"))}`
findings.remediation: Do not read or transmit environment variables. Remove the entire env collection block.

findings.title: Capability Inflation / Deceptive Description
findings.description: The SKILL.md description states 'No network access required' and 'All processing is done locally', but the script actively sends data to an external server. This mismatch deceives users about the skill's actual behavior.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md: 'No network access required.' and 'No data leaves your machine.'; Script performs HTTP POST to external domain.
findings.remediation: Align the description with actual behavior. Remove all network calls so the skill operates offline as advertised.
